problems.title: Find the largest number in a list
hints: Assume the first element numbers[0] is the largest
Loop through the list with a for loop
If the current number is greater than largest, update largest
Return largest after the loop ends — not inside it
problems.explanation: This is called a linear scan — O(n) time. You touch each element once and track the biggest. No built-in max() or sort() needed. The pattern: assume first, compare, update — appears in sorting algorithms everywhere.

problems.title: Count vowels in a string
problems.description: Given a string, count how many vowels (a,e,i,o,u) it contains. Case insensitive - 'A' and 'a' both count
hints: Create a variable containing all vowels: 'aeiou'
Create a counter variable starting at 0
Loop through each letter in the string
Check if the letter is in your vowels variable
Return the counter after the loop
problems.explanation: This teaches string iteration and the 'in' keyword. In Python you can check membership with 'if x in collection' - works on strings, lists, and more.

problems.title: Reverse a list
problems.description: Given a list of numbers, reverse it in place without using slicing [::-1] or reverse().
hints: Use two pointers — left starting at 0, right starting at len(numbers)-1
Swap numbers[left] and numbers[right] using a, b = b, a syntax
Move left up by 1 and right down by 1 after each swap
Stop when left >= right — they've met in the middle
problems.explanation: This is the two pointer technique — one of the most common patterns in DSA. Two pointers moving toward each other is used in sorting, searching, and string problems constantly.
